definitions: A programming language built at Google to solve real frustrations — slow build times, complex codebases, and difficulty writing concurrent software
Simple syntax: minimal, clean, inspired by C but without the complexity
Compiled & fast: compiles directly to machine code, fast like C/C++ but compiles much faster
Statically typed: types checked at compile time, but type inference (x := 5) keeps it less verbose
Garbage collected: memory managed automatically — no manual allocation/free like in C
Built-in concurrency: goroutines (lightweight threads) and channels (communication between them) make concurrent programming easy
Strong standard library: excellent built-in packages for HTTP servers, JSON, file I/O, testing — often no third-party libraries needed

types.name: Backend web servers & APIs
types.description: Fast, efficient servers and REST APIs

types.name: Cloud infrastructure tools
types.description: Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform are all written in Go

types.name: Microservices
types.description: Small, independent services that work together
